explicit_text: I currently live in Snohomish, Washington. This is where I am based while working as the Executive Vice President of SEIU Healthcare 1199 Northwest and continuing my nursing career. Living here grounds me as I balance my roles in the labor movement and my personal life as a woman of color. Snohomish is the place I call home while I navigate these responsibilities.
implicit_text: I currently live in a small city in Washington state. This is where I am based while working as the Executive Vice President of SEIU Healthcare 1199 Northwest and continuing my nursing career. Living here grounds me as I balance my roles in the labor movement and my personal life as a woman of color. This city is the place I call home while I navigate these responsibilities.

explicit_text: I have been a member of the Service Employees International Union, specifically SEIU Healthcare 1199 Northwest, since 2000. As the Executive Vice President, I spend my days advocating for healthcare workers, ensuring they have the resources and protections they need. During the COVID-19 pandemic, SEIU was vital in pushing for proper PPE like N95 masks and fighting for equitable treatment of all healthcare workers, from nurses to housekeepers. The union has been my platform to fight for racial and workplace justice throughout my 21 years as a nurse and union leader.
implicit_text: I have been a member of a major labor union representing healthcare workers, specifically the local healthcare division in the Northwest, since 2000. As the Executive Vice President, I spend my days advocating for healthcare workers, ensuring they have the resources and protections they need. During the COVID-19 pandemic, this organization was vital in pushing for proper PPE like N95 masks and fighting for equitable treatment of all healthcare workers, from nurses to housekeepers. The union has been my platform to fight for racial and workplace justice throughout my 21 years as a nurse and union leader.

entity: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
explicit_text: At the start of the COVID-19 pandemic, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) was the main authority providing guidelines to healthcare workers. However, there was confusion because the CDC initially described the virus as spread by droplets, while we believed it was airborne. This discrepancy made it challenging to secure the right protective equipment for our members. We had to push hard against these guidelines to ensure all workers received the highest level of protection, including N95 masks.
implicit_text: At the start of the COVID-19 pandemic, the primary federal public health agency was the main authority providing guidelines to healthcare workers. However, there was confusion because this agency initially described the virus as spread by droplets, while we believed it was airborne. This discrepancy made it challenging to secure the right protective equipment for our members. We had to push hard against these guidelines to ensure all workers received the highest level of protection, including N95 masks.

explicit_text: The N95 mask became a critical symbol and tool of protection for our healthcare workers during the pandemic. Early on, our union fought tirelessly to secure N95 masks for all hospital workers, not just nurses but also housekeepers and other staff. We knew that everyone needed the highest level of protection regardless of their role or pay grade. This fight involved legislative advocacy, public actions, and negotiations with employers to ensure equitable access to N95 masks for all members.
implicit_text: This particular respirator became a critical symbol and tool of protection for our healthcare workers during the pandemic. Early on, our union fought tirelessly to secure these masks for all hospital workers, not just nurses but also housekeepers and other staff. We knew that everyone needed the highest level of protection regardless of their role or pay grade. This fight involved legislative advocacy, public actions, and negotiations with employers to ensure equitable access to these respirators for all members.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic dramatically increased the demands of my work as a nurse and union leader. Starting in early 2020, our members faced uncertainty about job security, patient care, and safety equipment. We had to multiply our efforts to provide resources, fight for proper PPE like N95 masks, and advocate for paid time off and mental health support. The pandemic exposed racial and economic disparities in healthcare, which we addressed through union activism and public awareness campaigns.
implicit_text: The global health crisis that began in early 2020 dramatically increased the demands of my work as a nurse and union leader. Starting then, our members faced uncertainty about job security, patient care, and safety equipment. We had to multiply our efforts to provide resources, fight for proper PPE like N95 masks, and advocate for paid time off and mental health support. This crisis exposed racial and economic disparities in healthcare, which we addressed through union activism and public awareness campaigns.

explicit_text: The Black Lives Matter movement has been a long-standing fight for racial justice, and during the spring of 2020, it gained widespread attention alongside the COVID-19 pandemic. As a Black woman and union leader, I see the movement as crucial in highlighting the racial disparities that the pandemic made even more visible. It has influenced how we approach equity in healthcare and labor rights, reminding us that racial justice is essential to protecting all workers and communities.

As a Black woman and union leader, I see this movement as crucial in highlighting the racial disparities that the crisis made even more visible. It has influenced how we approach equity in healthcare and labor rights, reminding us that racial justice is essential to protecting all workers and communities.

explicit_text: Washington state is where I have lived and worked throughout my nursing and union career. It is the place where SEIU Healthcare 1199 Northwest operates and where I have been advocating for healthcare workers since 2000. The state’s political landscape has been a key arena for our efforts to secure protections like N95 masks and support for workers during the COVID-19 pandemic. Washington has been both my home and the community I serve in labor and healthcare.

This state has been both my home and the community I serve in labor and healthcare.
